reasoning: I start with A=10, B=5, Y=15. I will increase A to test if Y changes linearly with A.

I have observed that when A increases from 10 to 20 (delta A = +10), Y increases from 15 to 25 (delta Y = +10). This suggests a linear relationship Y = A + B, since 10 + 5 = 15 and 20 + 5 = 25. To confirm this hypothesis and rule out other possibilities, I will change B while keeping A constant. I will decrease B to see if Y decreases by the same amount.

I have observed two data points: (A=10, B=5, Y=15) and (A=20, B=5, Y=25) which suggests Y = A + B. Then I observed (A=20, B=0, Y=20). Let's verify: 20 + 0 = 20, which matches Y. The relationship Y = A + B holds for all observed points. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the equation.
